Page.title: Live Gold Prices Today — USD, JOD, SAR, AED, EGP
Page.description: Track live gold, silver, platinum & palladium prices in USD, Jordanian Dinar, Saudi Riyal, UAE Dirham and Egyptian Pound across 24K, 21K, 18K and 14K karats. Updated every minute on Tibr (تبر).
Page.intro: Tibr (<ar>تبر</ar>) — live spot prices for gold, silver, platinum and palladium across the Arab world. USD, JOD, SAR, AED, EGP. Refreshed every minute from FOREXCOM.
Page.aboutH2: About Tibr — Live Gold for the Arab World
Page.aboutP1: Tibr (تبر) is the Arabic word for native gold — raw, unrefined, straight from the source. We bring that same transparency to live precious-metal pricing for MENA: pure spot data, no markups, no fluff.
Page.aboutP2: Tibr surfaces live spot prices for gold (XAU), silver (XAG), platinum (XPT) and palladium (XPD) alongside karat-derived rates (24K, 21K, 18K, 14K) — the four most-traded karats in Arab jewellery markets.

country.jordanNote: 21K is the standard in Jordanian souks.
country.saudiNote: Riyadh, Jeddah, Dammam — live SAR rates.
country.uaeNote: Dubai Gold Souk pricing in AED.

Page.footer: © {year} Tibr (تبر) · Live gold from the source · Prices for informational purposes only.

Faq.heading: Frequently Asked Questions
Faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about live gold prices on Tibr.

items.q: How often does Tibr update gold prices?
items.a: Live spot prices refresh every minute from the FOREXCOM XAUUSD feed via goldapi.io. Pages cache for 60 seconds at the edge so visitors always see fresh data without overloading the upstream API.
Tibr covers 24K (pure gold), 21K (87.5% pure — the dominant karat in Saudi Arabia and Jordan), 18K (75% pure — common across UAE and Egypt), and 14K (58.3% pure).
US Dollar (USD), Jordanian Dinar (JOD), Saudi Riyal (SAR), UAE Dirham (AED), and Egyptian Pound (EGP). FX rates are static — confirm with your bank before any transaction.

items.q: Where do the prices come from?
items.a: Spot price is sourced from goldapi.io, which aggregates the FOREXCOM:XAUUSD feed. Karat prices are derived from the spot using the standard purity ratios published by the World Gold Council.
Yes. Tibr is free for personal and informational use. Always confirm with a licensed jeweller or bullion dealer before buying or selling.
